mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations despite high rates.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stabilizes below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day equity returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations when industrial production rises.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances when the rate exceeds 5.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that directly translates into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations despite positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering immediate market sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold condition shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.
